Title: Salicylic acid receptors activate jasmonic acid signalling through a non-canonical pathway to promote effector-triggered immunity

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes jasmonic acid signalling and de novo JA/JA-Ile accumulation during RPS2-mediated effector-triggered immunity by facilitating JAZ repressor reduction; required for full ETI-associated programmed cell death and pathogen resistance.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to strengthen immune responses by coordinating salicylic acid and jasmonic acid defence signalling; deployment should consider immunity context dependence and possible pleiotropy from altered SA signalling.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Salicylic acid receptor; BTB domain-containing Cullin3 E3 ligase adaptor with ankyrin repeats
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: npr3 npr4 mutant analysis; qRT-PCR of JA-responsive genes; co-immunoprecipitation; yeast two-hybrid; split-luciferase assay; pull-down assay; JAZ1 protein stability assay; hormone profiling; pathogen growth and ion leakage assays; MeJA rescue
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Psm ES4326 carrying avrRpt2; SA treatment; MeJA treatment; MG115 proteasome inhibitor
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: NPR4; JAZ1; JAZ2; JAZ4; JAZ5; JAZ6; JAZ8; JAZ9; JAZ10; JAZ11; NPR1
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter (for NPR3-GFP and transgenic assay contexts)
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: npr3 npr4 background shows altered basal resistance due to NPR1 accumulation; engineering NPR3 may perturb SA pathway balance and defence crosstalk.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts in a non-canonical JA activation pathway independent of COI1 at the early ETI stage; SA enhances interaction with JAZ proteins; both NPR3 and NPR4 contribute redundantly.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: JA/JA-Ile amplify defence signalling, promote ETI-associated programmed cell death, and help maintain defence against necrotrophs during ETI.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NPR3 is required together with NPR4 for early ETI-induced activation of JA-responsive genes. 2. NPR3 interacts with multiple JAZ repressors, with most interactions enhanced or requiring SA. 3. NPR3 contributes to ETI-triggered JAZ1 reduction and promotes subsequent JA/JA-Ile accumulation and resistance.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functions at early stage of ETI after avrRpt2 challenge

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes jasmonic acid signalling and ETI-associated de novo JA/JA-Ile synthesis by facilitating JAZ repressor reduction during RPS2-mediated ETI; required for full ETI-associated programmed cell death and resistance.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering node for coordinated activation of SA- and JA-linked immunity; manipulation should be carefully balanced because NPR4 also participates broadly in SA receptor biology.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Salicylic acid receptor; BTB domain-containing Cullin3 E3 ligase adaptor with ankyrin repeats
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: npr3 npr4 mutant analysis; qRT-PCR of JA-responsive genes; co-immunoprecipitation; split-luciferase assay; pull-down assay; JAZ1 protein stability assay; hormone profiling; pathogen growth and ion leakage assays; MeJA rescue
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Psm ES4326 carrying avrRpt2; SA treatment; MeJA treatment; MG115 proteasome inhibitor
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: NPR3; JAZ1; JAZ4; JAZ6; JAZ8; JAZ9; JAZ11; NPR1
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter (for NPR4-GFP and transgenic assay contexts)
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutating NPR4 with NPR3 causes severe ETI deficiency and altered basal resistance due to NPR1 accumulation; pathway engineering may have broad defence-network consequences.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts with NPR3 in a non-canonical JA activation pathway independent of COI1 at early ETI stage; several NPR4-JAZ interactions are SA enhanced in planta.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: JA/JA-Ile amplify defence signalling, promote ETI-associated programmed cell death, and help avoid vulnerability to necrotrophs during ETI.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NPR4 is jointly required with NPR3 for early ETI-induced activation of JA-responsive genes. 2. NPR4 interacts with JAZ proteins and contributes to ETI-triggered JAZ1 reduction. 3. NPR4 supports later JA/JA-Ile accumulation and full ETI resistance.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functions at early stage of ETI after avrRpt2 challenge

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a transcriptional repressor whose reduction during early ETI is required for JA-responsive gene activation; overexpression or stabilized jaz1Δjas impairs ETI-associated cell death and resistance.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: JAZ1 is a negative target for editing or controlled destabilization to enhance JA-linked immunity, but constitutive derepression could cause defence-growth tradeoffs.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: JASMONATE ZIM-domain protein; contains NT, ZIM and Jas domains
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: jasmonic acid signalling output; jasmonoyl-isoleucine-associated response
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 35S:HA-JAZ1 overexpression; jaz1Δjas transgenic line; qRT-PCR; co-immunoprecipitation; pull-down assay; split-luciferase; protein stability assay with pathogen, SA, JA and MG115; ion leakage and pathogen growth assays
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Psm ES4326 carrying avrRpt2; SA treatment; MeJA treatment; MG115 proteasome inhibitor; cycloheximide
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced early during ETI at 4 h post inoculation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Stabilized or overexpressed JAZ1 represses JA signalling and compromises ETI; conversely excessive destabilization may overactivate JA responses with possible growth costs.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reduction during ETI depends on SA, NPR3/4 and the 26S proteasome, but not simply on exogenous SA treatment; COI1-independent at early ETI stage.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: JA signalling promotes ETI-associated programmed cell death and immune amplification.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. JAZ1 is an ETI repressor, as its overexpression or stabilized ΔJas form impairs PCD and resistance. 2. NPR3/4 physically associate with JAZ1 in an SA-enhanced manner. 3. JAZ1 protein is reduced during early ETI in an SA-, NPR3/4- and proteasome-dependent manner.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Early induced transcript at 4 h after avrRpt2 inoculation

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Canonical jasmonate receptor required for full ETI-associated programmed cell death and resistance amplification, but dispensable for the early ETI-induced JA-responsive transcriptional burst.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for maintaining canonical JA signal amplification during immune responses; not sufficient to explain early ETI activation, so engineering should consider non-canonical NPR3/4 route.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: F-box protein; SCF-type jasmonate receptor component
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: coi1 mutant analysis; qRT-PCR of JA-responsive genes; ion leakage assay; pathogen growth assay; MeJA rescue interpretation
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Psm ES4326 carrying avrRpt2; MeJA treatment
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Defective COI1 compromises ETI-associated PCD and resistance; broader JA-insensitive phenotypes likely.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Study specifically shows early ETI JA signalling occurs independently of COI1, but later canonical amplification still matters.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: JA-Ile perception by COI1 amplifies immune signalling and contributes to ETI defence.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. coi1 mutants are compromised in ETI-associated PCD and pathogen restriction. 2. Early ETI induction of JA-responsive genes still occurs in coi1, indicating a non-canonical pathway upstream. 3. Canonical COI1 signalling likely contributes to subsequent amplification of JA defence during ETI.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts fatty acid hydroperoxides produced by lipoxygenase into unstable allene oxides in the jasmonate biosynthetic pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: AOS transcript is induced during ETI
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used genetically as JA-deficient mutant aos to test JA requirement in ETI; not a primary mechanistic focus beyond demonstrating JA biosynthesis requirement.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOS expression is induced during RPS2-mediated ETI. 2. aos mutants show reduced ETI-associated PCD and reduced resistance to Psm ES4326/avrRpt2. 3. Early ETI induction of JA-responsive genes is not abolished in aos, indicating de novo JA synthesis is not required for the initial non-canonical activation step.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; aos mutant ion leakage assay; pathogen growth assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports maintaining JA biosynthetic capacity for effective ETI; however, the paper does not position AOS as the major engineering target compared with NPR3/4-JAZ regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced at 4 h after avrRpt2 inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of AOS compromises immunity-associated PCD and resistance.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxygenates polyunsaturated fatty acids to generate fatty acid hydroperoxides that feed jasmonate biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: polyunsaturated fatty acids such as α-linolenic acid
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced early at 4 h during ETI in WT; induction reduced in rps2, sid2 and npr3 npr4
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Serves here primarily as a JA-responsive/readout biosynthetic gene linking early signalling to later de novo JA production.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOX3 is rapidly induced during RPS2-mediated ETI. 2. Its induction requires SA and NPR3/4 but not NPR1 or COI1 at the early stage. 3. LOX3 supports the model that early non-canonical signalling primes de novo JA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream marker or target to enhance JA production during ETI, but the study emphasizes upstream NPR3/4-mediated control.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Early induced at 4 h after avrRpt2 inoculation

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces 12-oxophytodienoic acid in the jasmonate biosynthetic pathway to advance synthesis toward jasmonic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: OPC-8:0 / reduced OPDA derivative en route to JA
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Oxophytodienoate reductase / old yellow enzyme family
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced during ETI in WT and reduced in rps2
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as one of the JA-responsive genes examined in early ETI induction.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OPR3 transcript is induced during ETI downstream of RPS2. 2. This supports activation of the JA biosynthetic program during ETI. 3. The gene is part of the broader de novo JA synthesis wave following SA/NPR3/4-mediated early signalling.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could support JA production if enhanced, but not identified as a principal control gene in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced at 4 h after avrRpt2 inoculation
